7.anchor: However, he has given each one of us a special gift

8.anchor: and gave gifts to his people.”

9.anchor: Notice that it says “he ascended.” This clearly means that Christ also descended to our lowly world.
9.note: Some manuscripts read to the lower parts of the earth.

26.anchor: And “don’t sin by letting anger control you.”

30.anchor: And do not bring sorrow to God’s Holy Spirit by the way you live. Remember, he has identified you as his own,
30.note: Or has put his seal on you.

1.anchor: When I think of all this, I, Paul, a prisoner of Christ Jesus for the benefit of you Gentiles
1.note: Paul resumes this thought in verse 14: “When I think of all this, I fall to my knees and pray to the Father.”

6.anchor: And this is God’s plan: Both Gentiles and Jews who believe the Good News share equally in the riches inherited by God’s children. Both are part of the same body, and both enjoy the promise of blessings because they belong to Christ Jesus.
6.note: Or because they are united with Christ Jesus.

9.anchor: I was chosen to explain to everyone
9.note: Some manuscripts do not include to everyone.

12.anchor: Because of Christ and our faith in him,

14.anchor: When I think of all this, I fall to my knees and pray to the Father,
14.note: Some manuscripts read the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ.

15.anchor: the Creator of everything in heaven and on earth.
15.note: Or from whom every family in heaven and on earth takes its name.

1.anchor: Children, obey your parents because you belong to the Lord,
1.note: Or Children, obey your parents who belong to the Lord; Children, obey your parents.

2.anchor: If you honor your father and mother, “things will go well for you, and you will have a long life on the earth.”

15.anchor: For shoes, put on the peace that comes from the Good News so that you will be fully prepared.
15.note: Or For shoes, put on the readiness to preach the Good News of peace with God.

16.anchor: In addition to all of these, hold up the shield of faith to stop the fiery arrows of the devil.

18.anchor: Pray in the Spirit at all times and on every occasion. Stay alert and be persistent in your prayers for all believers everywhere.
18.note: Greek all of God’s holy people.

19.anchor: And pray for me, too. Ask God to give me the right words so I can boldly explain God’s mysterious plan that the Good News is for Jews and Gentiles alike.
19.note: Greek explain the mystery of the Good News; explain the mystery.

23.anchor: Peace be with you, dear brothers and sisters,

2.anchor: Live a life filled with love, following the example of Christ. He loved us

26.anchor: to make her holy and clean, washed by the cleansing of God’s word.
26.note: Greek washed by water with the word.

31.anchor: As the Scriptures say, “A man leaves his father and mother and is joined to his wife, and the two are united into one.”

2.anchor: You used to live in sin, just like the rest of the world, obeying the devil—the commander of the powers in the unseen world.
2.note: Greek obeying the commander of the power of the air.

1.anchor: I am writing to God’s holy people in Ephesus,
1.note: The most ancient manuscripts do not include in Ephesus.

6.anchor: So we praise God for the glorious grace he has poured out on us who belong to his dear Son.
6.note: Greek to us in the beloved.

11.anchor: Furthermore, because we are united with Christ, we have received an inheritance from God,
11.note: Or we have become God’s inheritance.

13.anchor: And now you Gentiles have also heard the truth, the Good News that God saves you. And when you believed in Christ, he identified you as his own
13.note: Or he put his seal on you.

15.anchor: Ever since I first heard of your strong faith in the Lord Jesus and your love for God’s people everywhere,
15.note: Some manuscripts read your faithfulness to the Lord Jesus and to God’s people everywhere.

17.anchor: asking God, the glorious Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, to give you spiritual wisdom
17.note: Or to give you the Spirit of wisdom.

18.anchor: I pray that your hearts will be flooded with light so that you can understand the confident hope he has given to those he called—his holy people who are his rich and glorious inheritance.
18.note: Or called, and the rich and glorious inheritance he has given to his holy people.
